name: BEFCOR Business Expansion Funding Corporation
description_short: Non-profit SBA 504 lender serving small businesses across North Carolina and select South Carolina counties with fixed-rate financing for real estate, construction, and expansion.
description_long:
BEFCOR (Business Expansion Funding Corporation) is a mission-driven, non-profit small business lender founded over 40 years ago with deep roots in North Carolina's entrepreneurial community. The organization is structured as an SBA-licensed Certified Development Company (CDC) and a US Department of the Treasury-certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI), giving it dual authority to serve underserved small business borrowers across all 100 North Carolina counties plus four South Carolina counties (Chester, Chesterfield, Lancaster, and York).

BEFCOR specializes in SBA 504 loans—a product available exclusively through CDCs—designed for small business owners seeking long-term, fixed-rate financing for real estate purchases, construction, and business expansion. Their current rates (as of 2025) range from 5.61% for 10-year terms to 5.78% for 20-year terms. As a CDFI, they also offer direct small business loans up to $150,000 in North Carolina for borrowers who cannot secure full financing from traditional banks or credit unions. The organization emphasizes favorable loan terms including lower down payments, longer repayment periods, and competitive rates.

What distinguishes BEFCOR is its non-profit structure and explicit mission focus: the organization prioritizes job creation and community economic development over shareholder returns. They transparently state what they are not (a bank, government agency, profit-driven entity, or grant provider), positioning themselves as ethical lenders offering sound financial guidance. In 2025 alone, they approved over $80 million in loans that created more than 1,000 new jobs. Their website emphasizes a straightforward, transparent process and highlight testimonials from satisfied borrowers describing simple, well-explained transactions.

The main caveat is geographic limitation: BEFCOR serves only North Carolina (all counties) and four specific South Carolina counties. Additionally, as a CDC/CDFI lender, they specialize in SBA 504 and small-dollar CDFI products rather than offering the full spectrum of business financing (merchant cash advances, equipment loans, lines of credit). The organization is transparent about its mission focus, which may result in stricter underwriting than some commercial lenders.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Non-profit structure with explicit mission to support job creation and community development—no shareholder pressure for profit maximization
Dual SBA CDC and Treasury-certified CDFI credentials provide exclusive access to SBA 504 loans (only available through CDCs) and direct CDFI lending up to $150,000
Long-term fixed rates with no rate volatility: 5.61%-5.78% depending on term, locking in predictable payments for 10, 20, or 25 years
Lower down payment requirements and longer repayment periods compared to traditional bank financing
40+ years of established lending history in North Carolina with $80+ million in 2025 loan approvals supporting 1,000+ new jobs
Transparent process explicitly stating what they are not, plus free resource connections for small business owners
Serves all 100 North Carolina counties plus four South Carolina counties, providing broad geographic access
cons: Geographic limitation: only serves North Carolina (all counties) and four specific South Carolina counties—no coverage outside this region
Limited product range: specializes primarily in SBA 504 and CDFI loans, not equipment financing, merchant cash advances, or other alternative business products

Mission-focused underwriting may result in stricter qualification criteria than commercial lenders; explicitly serves businesses that 'cannot obtain full financing from a bank or credit union,' implying selective approval
As a non-profit CDC, may have slower processing timelines compared to commercial lenders focused on loan velocity
No information provided on maximum loan amounts for SBA 504 products, making it unclear if there's a cap above which they cannot finance
best_for: Small business owners in North Carolina or four South Carolina counties seeking $150K-$1M+ financing for real estate, construction, or major expansion who were declined by traditional banks
Entrepreneurs who value mission-driven lending and want financing structured to preserve working capital with long-term fixed rates and lower down payments
Established small businesses seeking to refinance or access capital for growth without giving up equity or dealing with fast-term repayment structures
Community-focused business owners who prioritize job creation and want a lender aligned with economic development goals
diagnosis: BEFCOR is best for small business owners in North Carolina or four South Carolina counties who need affordable, mission-aligned long-term financing for real estate, construction, or expansion and either were declined by traditional banks or want fixed-rate predictability. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation (North Carolina + 4 SC counties only) and a product focus on SBA 504 and CDFI loans rather than the full spectrum of alternative business financing.
services: SBA 504 loans for real estate purchases, construction, and business expansion
CDFI direct loans up to $150,000 for qualifying North Carolina businesses
Business financing consultation and application guidance
Free resource connections and referrals for small business support services
Loan management and servicing for existing borrowers
Financial guidance and transparency throughout the loan process
Partnership programs for lenders seeking to deliver SBA 504 and CDFI solutions to their clients
Support for businesses unable to obtain full financing from traditional banks or credit unions
Working capital preservation strategies through lower monthly payments and longer amortization
Business growth financing across all industries in covered geographic regions
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
